beats.narration_text: Hei — Liam, in for Bear. audience-preset: one finished reel, different brands. Fork the canonical beat sheet, rewrite the narration register, swap the outro, rebuild under the brand palette and voice. The canonical beat sheet is never touched. Current brands: HAI Pragmatist, Medhavy Wonder, Northeastern Lecture, NikBearBrown Teardown.

props.runningText: fork sheet → rewrite register → swap outro → build under brand...
output: Narration rewritten in brand register (same facts, different voice)
Brands: hai | medhavy | neu | nbb | musinique

beats.narration_text: Anatomy. One canonical reel. N brand variants. Each variant is a namespaced copy — beat sheet dot brand dot json — that shares the facts but rewrites the register. Register is the voice posture: Pragmatist says what the tool does and why you would use it today; Wonder asks what the learner finds surprising; Teardown deconstructs how it was built; Lecture presents the concept for a course context. The brand data file — brands slash brand dot md — is the sole source of register, voice, palette, outro, and any extra brand laws.

props.title: One reel. N brand variants. Same facts, different registers.
files: SKILL.md — the flow (identical for every brand) + brand table
brands/hai.md — HAI Pragmatist: register, voice, palette, outro
brands/medhavy.md — Medhavy Wonder: Okabe-Ito palette, af_kore voice
props.calloutSub: register + voice + palette + outro, all in one file

beats.narration_text: Pipeline. Step one: fork the sheet — brand variant dot py writes beat sheet dot brand dot json from the canonical sheet. The canonical is never modified. Step two: rewrite the register — same beats, same facts, narration voice changes. Step three: optional tangent — at most one brand-flavored beat if the brand file calls for it. Step four: swap the outro. Step five: build namespaced — audio, renders, and composite run under the brand namespace with the brand's palette and voice.

props.title: Fork → rewrite register → tangent → outro → build.

phases.label: REGISTER
phases.note: rewrite narration in brand's register (Pragmatist/Wonder/Teardown/Lecture); same facts

phases.label: TANGENT
phases.note: optional: at most one brand-flavored beat if brands/<brand>.md calls for it

phases.label: OUTRO
phases.note: swap the outro beat for the brand's outro (HAI | Medhavy | NU | NikBearBrown)

phases.label: BUILD
phases.note: audio + renders + composite under brand namespace; palette from brand VOX_PALETTE key

props.footerNote: Same publish path as any reel. The original reel is unaffected.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo. Step two, register rewrite, is purely free — no audio, no build, no brand variant script. Watch Claude take one mechanism beat narration and rewrite it in two registers: HAI Pragmatist and Medhavy Wonder. Same facts, different voice.

props.runningText: same facts → Pragmatist register → Wonder register...
output: HAI rewrite: Pragmatist — tool-first, what + why today
Medhavy rewrite: Wonder — question-first, what surprises you
No audio, no build, no paid spend

beats.narration_text: Source beat — canonical Liam register. The canonical beat sheet is never modified. brand variant dot py forks it into a namespaced copy; the copy is where all brand changes live. HAI Pragmatist rewrite: one script, one command. Brand variant forks your canonical reel into a clean copy. Narration rewrites to Pragmatist register. Palette, voice, and outro swap in. Your original reel is untouched. Medhavy Wonder rewrite: Have you noticed that the same explainer video can reach two completely different audiences without changing a single fact? That is what audience-preset does — it rewrites the voice, not the content. The wonder is in how little needs to change.

the copy is where all brand changes live."

into a clean copy. Narration rewrites to Pragmatist
register. Palette, voice, and outro swap in.
Your original reel is untouched."

MEDHAVY rewrite (Wonder — question-first, surprising):

That is what audience-preset does — it rewrites

little needs to change."

Same facts. Same beats. Different registers.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one. One skill, one flow, N brands. The per-brand facts — register, voice, palette key, outro, tangent flavor, any brand laws — all live in brands slash brand dot md. The flow is identical for every brand. Brand variant dot py reads that file, scaffolds the variant, and the rest of the pipeline is unchanged. Adding a brand is adding a data file.

props.heading: Every brand is a data file. No code changes.
props.body: brands/<brand>.md holds: register name + description, voice env var (ELEVENLABS_VOICE_*), voice_kokoro default, VOX_PALETTE key, outro beat spec, tangent flavor, any extra brand laws. brand_variant.py takes the brand name as an argument and reads from that file. The pipeline (fork → rewrite → tangent → outro → build) is unchanged for every brand. Adding a brand = adding brands/<name>.md with the same fields.
props.quote: One skill, one flow, N brands.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two. The canonical beat sheet is never modified. The fork step runs brand variant dot py, which scaffolds a copy — beat sheet dot brand dot json — from the canonical. All brand changes happen in the copy. If you run audience-preset twice on the same reel with two different brands, you get two independent copies; the canonical is untouched by both. The original reel is always the publish-ready source.

props.heading: The canonical is the source. The variant is the copy.
props.body: brand_variant.py reads the canonical beat_sheet.json → writes beat_sheet.<brand>.json (sibling file) or a new <brand>-<slug>/ directory (for hai and medhavy). The canonical is read-only for this operation. Two brands = two independent variant files. The canonical beat_sheet.json is also the revert target if a variant drifts — you always have a clean source to fork from again.
props.quote: The canonical beat_sheet.json is NEVER modified.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three. No code changes — brand variant dot py takes the brand name as an argument. The pipeline reads from the brand data file and the rest of the pipeline is unchanged. Adding a brand means adding a data file with the same fields: register, voice, palette key, outro, optional tangent flavor, optional extra brand laws. There is no new code path for a new brand.

props.heading: Add a brand by adding a data file.
props.body: To add a brand: create brands/<name>.md with the same fields as the existing brand files (register, voice env var, voice_kokoro, VOX_PALETTE key, outro spec, tangent flavor, any brand laws). Run brand_variant.py <reel> <name> — done. No changes to brand_variant.py, no changes to compile.py, no changes to any runtime script. The data file IS the code.
props.quote: No code changes — brand_variant.py takes the brand name as an argument.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Claude, Branded. audience-preset delivers non-destructive brand variants: the canonical reel is never touched, the variant rewrites the register and palette, and adding a new brand is adding a data file.

artifactLines: canonical beat_sheet.json is NEVER modified — variant is always a copy
register rewrite: same facts, different voice (Pragmatist/Wonder/Teardown/Lecture)
brand = brands/<name>.md — palette, voice, outro, laws all in one file
adding a new brand = adding a data file, no code changes

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run audience-preset on any finished reel. Two questions before you start: which brand — and have you confirmed the canonical beat sheet is clean and untouched before forking?

props.runningText: brand chosen? canonical untouched before fork?
